title: Neznámý titulek
content: The April 2026 update has been applied to the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings and the full standings can be found here. The biggest changes this month came in the gi divisions and they were all thanks to the latest stop on the IBJJF grand slam circuit, the IBJJF Pan Championship 2026. There were a couple of changes in the no gi rankings as well but they came from a wide range of events taking place all around the world. Polaris 36 kicked things off with some of Europe’s finest but UFC BJJ 6 followed quickly after with several title-fights, and Who’s Number One 32 featured a lightweight grand prix right at the end of the month. The Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings also have their own official sponsor, our proud partners REVGEAR! You can find out more about REVGEAR Jiu Jitsu and take a look at all of fantastic the products they offer at either www.revgear.com or www.revgeareurope.co.uk, and you can drop code Jits20 into the checkout for 20% off regular priced items. Table of Contents Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings – April 2026 Update Men’s No Gi BJJ Rankings Bantamweight – Under 135lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Featherweight – Under 148lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Lightweight – Under 162lbs Dorian Olivarez became the new Who’s Number One lightweight world champion with a pair of big wins and he has now re-entered the rankings at number four as a result. Welterweight – Under 175lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Middleweight – Under 190lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Light-Heavyweight – Under 205lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Heavyweight – Under 225lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Super-Heavyweight – Over 225lbs Elder Cruz might have ended up with a draw at UFC BJJ 6 but it was against one of the very best in the world, so he actually moved up in the rankings and now sits at 11th. Pound for Pound There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Jits Magazine Men’s Gi BJJ Rankings Bantamweight – Under 137lbs Marcos Gomes beat two of the top three in this division at the IBJJF Pan Championship and he is now the new number one as a result. Featherweight – Under 154lbs Shoya Ishiguro left with a silver medal at Pans but after a big win he still moves up and into the 7th spot, while Samuel Nagai also won silver and is now the new number three. Cole Abate was the man who beat him though, and he advances one place up to 2nd. Lightweight – Under 169lbs Will Wilson continued his breakthrough year and he has advanced two places into 6th, while newcomer Gabriel Cardoso slots in right behind him in 7th, Welterweight – Under 181lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Middleweight – Under 194lbs Enderson Dias won gold at Pans and breaks back into the rankings at 10th, while silver medalist Alex Munis is a newcomer at 11th and Tommy Langaker hits the gi rankings for the first time right behind him in 12th. Light-Heavyweight – Under 208lbs Luiz Felipe Santos won his first major tournament at Pans and although his division didn’t have some of the biggest names at this weight, it had enough tough competition to see him enter the rankings at number nine. Heavyweight – Under 225lbs Nolan Stuart has earned the number one spot once again with a big win at Pans, reaffirming his place at the very top of the sport. Super-Heavyweight – Over 225lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Pound for Pound Nolan Stuart climbs up to number six here thanks to returning to the number one spot at heavyweight, while new bantamweight king Marcos Gomes slots in behind him in 7th. Jits Magazine Women’s No Gi BJJ Rankings Strawweight – Under 115lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Featherweight – Under 125lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Lightweight – Under 135lbs Cassia Moura scored the biggest win of her career at UFC BJJ 6 and not only is she now their bantamweight champion, she also takes the top spot in these rankings. She wasn’t the only one either, Anna Rodrigues got a big win too and she returns in the 12th spot. Middleweight – Under 146lbs Lillian Marchand announced her arrival in the middleweight no gi rankings with an impressive performance at WNO 32, earning herself the position at number thirteen. Heavyweight – Over 146lbs Kendall Reusing made her return to professional Jiu-Jitsu competition by successfully defending her openweight title at Polaris 32, and she returns to the rankings at number seven. Pound for Pound Cassia Moura was the only newcomer here in at number five, but the other four women above her all moved up a spot due to her arrival. Jits Magazine Women’s Gi BJJ Rankings Strawweight – Under 118lbs Ana Lima Da Silva entered the rankings for the first time and rocketed all the way up to 3rd with several big wins at Pans, while Mariana Rolszt and Diana Teixeira both advance up the rankings to take up the 4th and 5th spots respectively. The only other change here came just above them, as Mayssa Bastos regained a place and is now 2nd. Featherweight – Under 129lbs The only change here came from Margot Ciccarelli, as she moves up two places and now occupies the 3rd spot at featherweight. Lightweight – Under 141lbs Sarah Galvao has now completed her rise to the top at lightweight after another major title, while the opposite end of the rankings here sees Sabrina Gondim return at number fifteen. Middleweight – Under 163lbs Elizabeth Mitrovic climbs all the way back up to 4th with a great performance at Pans and there’s another change a little further down, as Lillian Marchand breaks in to the gi rankings as well as no gi and is now number eight here. Heavyweight – Over 163lbs There were no changes in the April 2026 update for this division of the Jits Magazine BJJ Rankings. Pound for Pound Ana Lima Da Silva is the new number eight after her performance at Pans but the big news here is that there’s a new woman in the top spot. Sarah Galvao won both her weight and the absolute division at Pans, beating the previous pound for pound number one during her absolute run, so she earned her place at the top here. Tags: Alex MunisAna Lima Da SilvaAnna RodriguesBJJ Rankings UpdateCassia MouraCole AbateDiana TeixeiraDorian OlivarezElder CruzElizabeth MitrovicEnderson DiasGabriel CardosoKendall ReusingLillian MarchandLuiz Felipe SantosMarcos GomesMargot CiccarelliMariana RolsztMayssa BastosNolan StuartSabrina GondimSamuel NagaiSarah GalvaoShoya IshiguroTommy LangakerWill Wilson
